title: ceratocephala falcata subsp. incana
intro: Ceratocephala falcata subsp. incana, also known as the Hoary Ballhead, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ceratocephala falcata subsp. incana include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
